headline: Consumer and Industrials Lead a Cautious Friday Rally as Tech Retreats
body_html:
Equity markets closed modestly higher on Friday, July 10, with breadth tilting positive — 224 advancers against 169 decliners across the roughly 400-name universe — as investors positioned ahead of what promises to be a pivotal week of earnings, consumer-price data, and fresh Iran-related headlines. The session's dominant theme was a deliberate rotation out of high-multiple technology and software names and into more tangible, cyclical businesses, a pattern that analysts attributed partly to a Federal Reserve report flagging stepped-up inflation pressures from tariffs, the Iran conflict, and the AI buildout.

Consumer led all sectors with a gain of 1.11%, followed closely by Industrials at 0.97%. Homebuilders and Materials added modest fractions, while Healthcare, Software, and Semiconductors each shed roughly half a percent, dragged lower by sharp drops in identity-security and database software names. The standout mover of the session was Boise Cascade, the wood-products company, which surged 5.29% — a move that fit neatly into the broader Materials and timber complex bid, with fellow timber name Weyerhaeuser also advancing more than 4%.

Geopolitics remained an undercurrent throughout the day. Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz slowed amid renewed Iran tensions even as Washington and Tehran signaled a willingness to keep diplomatic channels open. Heading into next week, the market's ability to hold these cyclical gains may depend heavily on whether earnings beats can offset an increasingly complicated macro backdrop.

briefs.headline: Boise Cascade Surges to Top of the Tape in Timber Sweep
briefs.body: Wood-products manufacturer Boise Cascade rose 5.29% on Friday, the session's largest single-stock gain. Timber REIT Weyerhaeuser added 4.22%, suggesting broad-based demand for the lumber and forest-products complex. The paired move lifted the Materials sector to a 0.45% advance on the day.

briefs.headline: Software Stocks Bear the Brunt of the Tech Selloff
briefs.body: Identity-security firm Okta fell 6.86%, database platform MongoDB dropped 5.73%, and cybersecurity provider CrowdStrike slid 5.66%, making Software and Semiconductors the weakest sectors of the session. The selling appeared tied to rotation rather than company-specific news, as investors trimmed high-growth exposure ahead of a data-heavy week.

briefs.headline: Fed Inflation Warning Adds to Market Caution
briefs.body: A Federal Reserve report released ahead of the weekend cited notably higher inflation pressures stemming from tariffs, the ongoing Iran conflict, and the rapid buildout of AI infrastructure. The findings reinforced expectations that the rate-cutting path remains narrow, tempering enthusiasm in rate-sensitive parts of the market.

briefs.headline: US-Iran Diplomatic Window Stays Open Despite Ceasefire Collapse
briefs.body: President Trump confirmed Friday that the United States agreed to Iran's request to resume talks, even as he declared the earlier ceasefire over. The development offered a thin layer of geopolitical relief to markets while keeping oil-supply risk elevated, with the IEA warning that further escalation could threaten a projected 2027 crude surplus.

briefs.headline: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Slows, Helium Supply Chain at Risk
briefs.body: Shipping data showed reduced vessel movement through the Strait of Hormuz as Iran tensions reignited on Friday. Separately, China announced a temporary ban on helium exports amid the flare-up, adding a supply-chain dimension to the geopolitical story that could ripple into semiconductor and industrial-gas markets in coming weeks.

briefs.headline: Investors Brace for a Packed Calendar in the Week Ahead
briefs.body: Earnings season opens in earnest next week alongside a closely watched consumer-price-index release and an expected continuation of Iran-related headlines. Analysts cautioned that the combination of corporate guidance, inflation data, and geopolitical noise makes for an unusually high-uncertainty setup as the summer trading stretch begins.
